subject: math
activity_templates: A 1st-grade place-value worksheet using tens-and-ones blocks. Four problems; each shows groups of cartoon base-ten blocks (tens rods and unit cubes) with boxes for the child to write the number of tens, ones, and the total. {{character}} stands to the right.
An 'expanded form' worksheet. Four two-digit numbers in a column; the child rewrites each as '__ tens + __ ones = __'. {{character}} holds a big '10' sign.

A compare-numbers place-value worksheet with 5 pairs of two-digit numbers. Child writes <, >, or = in the box between them. Place-value chart printed across the top for reference. {{character}} cheers.

A build-a-number worksheet. Three friendly cartoon scenarios each give a digit count (e.g. '3 tens and 4 ones'); child writes the two-digit number and draws the blocks. {{character}} is building blocks.
